hook: On untrusted networks, cleartext is path-visible. TLS negotiates versions and cipher suites, presents certificates, derives session keys, then carries HTTP and APIs inside encrypted records.
cta: The padlock means transport encryption—not automatic trust in hostname, CA validation, or application security.

storyboardBeats.objective: Unencrypted traffic on untrusted networks can be passively observed before TLS protects the session.
Client sends ClientHello with supported versions and cipher suites; no application secrets yet.
Server responds with ServerHello and presents the certificate chain; trust requires client-side validation.
Key exchange and Finished messages establish session keys and a confidential, integrity-protected record layer.
Application payloads travel inside the encrypted TLS record layer where outsiders cannot read content.

narrationPlaceholders.scriptIntent: Chưa TLS — cleartext trên mạng không tin cậy có thể bị sniff.
ClientHello: version, cipher suites, extensions — chưa gửi secret ứng dụng.
ServerHello + certificate — trust do client validate, chưa khẳng định trên hình.
Finished — khóa phiên và record layer AEAD; kênh bí mật sẵn sàng.
Application Data — HTTP/API trong tunnel; ngoài path không đọc được payload.

transitionToNext.rationale: After TLS on the web, SSH applies the same handshake-and-trust pattern to remote shells—a natural next module for engineers.
